title: Damage delivery
principles: Damage delivery describes how an individual damage component reaches its application point and how that component applies damage at that point.
Delivery path and spatial application must remain separate because a component may combine projectile, hitscan, or beam travel with direct or radial application.
Hitscan, projectile, and beam classifications require explicit path evidence and must not be inferred by eliminating other possibilities.
Direct application affects an explicitly reached target, while radial application affects valid targets within a supplied spatial region.
Projectile delivery establishes travel but does not define speed, trajectory, gravity, homing, collision, or detonation behavior unless those properties are supplied.
Radial application does not by itself establish explosion behavior, uniform damage, multi-target reliability, or area-control function.
Each damage component must preserve its own delivery path, spatial application, statistics, and mechanical evidence.

hitscan_path: The component reaches its application point without conventional projectile travel.
This classification does not establish accuracy, target acquisition, guaranteed contact, or spatial application.
projectile_path: The component travels as a projectile before reaching its application point.
Travel time and trajectory remain undetermined unless supported by explicit projectile data.
beam_path: The component reaches its application point through beam delivery rather than conventional projectile travel.
Range, contact, and application behavior must be interpreted through beam_behavior.
direct_application: The component applies damage to an explicitly reached target or collision point.
Direct application does not determine whether its path is hitscan, projectile, beam, or another supported mechanism.
radial_application: The component applies damage within a supplied spatial region around an origin.
Radius and falloff must remain associated with this specific component.
undetermined_path: The supplied evidence does not identify how the component reaches its application point.
undetermined_application: The supplied evidence does not identify whether the component applies damage directly, radially, or through another spatial model.

direct_and_radial_components_present: Represent direct and radial damage as separate components when both are independently supplied.
Do not merge their damage, critical, status, radius, falloff, or target eligibility.
projectile_with_radial_component: Preserve projectile travel and radial application as separate properties or components.
Do not assume the radial component activates on first impact, after a delay, or through manual detonation without explicit evidence.
punch_through_present: Associate Punch Through only with the eligible delivery component.
Do not classify Punch Through as radial application or guaranteed multi-target damage.
multiple_components_present: Preserve a separate delivery profile for every component.
Do not assume that components occur simultaneously, share an origin, or affect the same target.

exceptions: Do not infer projectile speed, trajectory, gravity, homing, collision, or detonation behavior from projectile delivery alone.
Do not infer radius, explosion behavior, falloff, group-clear capability, or area control from an aoe or radial label alone.
Do not assume that separate components share damage, critical, status, targeting, or Punch Through behavior.
Do not infer delivery path or spatial application solely from the absence of another classification.
Do not let charge, burst, held input, or another attack-rhythm property redefine the resulting component's delivery profile.
